game_name: Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back

design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through film-faithful levels using melee and ranged attacks, switching between three characters with distinct abilities. Collect power-ups and weapons to overcome enemy gauntlets and environmental hazards.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Constant enemy pressure balanced against limited health and positioning-based platforming challenges. Reviewers noted 'cheap hits from enemies off screen' and 'blind jumps' creating friction.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Luke, Han, and Chewie in iconic Empire Strikes Back scenarios with cinematic presentation. Use character-specific abilities (Luke's Force powers and lightsaber blocking, Han's grenades, Chewie's spinning attack) to solve combat encounters.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commits to high difficulty and enemy density as core challenge philosophy. Rejects difficulty accessibility—reviewers explicitly praised harsh challenge. Emphasizes spectacle (Mode 7 sequences like AT-AT assault) alongside mechanical demanding gameplay. Prioritizes film authenticity over gameplay convenience.

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives system with configurable count (debug menu allows up to 99 lives). Password system allows save-scumming between levels, reducing per-level frustration.
difficulty.continues_system: Password/continue system provides level-based checkpoints, making individual level deaths less punishing than no-save runs.
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpointing via password system. No mid-level checkpoints within stages.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal. Difficulty is fixed per level. Password system and configurable lives (via debug) are external solutions rather than in-game difficulty modes. No selectable difficulty settings documented.

progression.upgrade_system: Character-specific power-ups that persist within a playthrough: Luke gains Force Powers after Dagobah mission; Han unlocks grenades; Chewie gains spinning attack. Primary and secondary weapon slots allow tactical switching. Health refill pickups.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression tied to story beats (Force Powers unlock after story milestone). New abilities appear as level-based pickups rather than unlocks. Hidden level select cheat exists but not documented as intended feature.
replayability_hooks: Three distinct playable characters with unique movesets encourage replays
Password system allows non-sequential replay of favorite levels
High difficulty creates skill mastery replay appeal

world_building_techniques: Cutscenes depicting film scenes (Ice Fields of Hoth scenes with generated character likenesses)
Level theming directly mirrors film locations (Dagobah, Hoth, etc.)
Mode 7 spectacle sequences (AT-AT assault) create cinematic impact
Character abilities tied to lore (Luke's lightsaber blocking, Force powers; Han's grenades)
Boss encounters with thematic weight (Darth Vader as final boss, faithful to film)

boss_design.philosophy: Climactic encounters aligned with film progression. Final boss is Darth Vader, positioning him as ultimate challenge. Boss design emphasizes pattern recognition and character-specific strategies.

faction_indicators: Rebel Alliance (player perspective, Hoth base, Luke's Force allegiance)
Empire forces (ubiquitous enemies, AT-AT walkers, Vader presence)
danger_signals: Enemy density and off-screen attacks signal hostile empire environment
Difficulty spikes and 'cheap hits' create tension around exploration safety
AT-AT sequences reframe player as vulnerable infiltrator
environmental_storytelling: Dagobah swamp conveys isolation and training setting
Ice Fields of Hoth establish frozen, sparse danger
Mode 7 vehicle sequences (AT-AT assault) visualize empire's technological dominance

sources_used: Wikipedia: Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (gameplay, development, reception)
The Cutting Room Floor: Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (debug features, development notes)
